title: Focus ring composed contrast 1.24:1 on white — WCAG SC 1.4.11 fail
impact: Every interactive component using the default focus ring will be nearly invisible to sighted keyboard users. Even at full opacity, primary-400 (#60A5FA) on white achieves only 2.54:1 — below the 3:1 SC 1.4.11 minimum. The focus.ring-opacity token of 0.25 composites the ring to #D7E9FE on white, yielding 1.24:1. Healthcare mandate: keyboard-only and low-vision users cannot identify focused controls.
evidence: focus.ring-color = var(--hx-color-primary-400) = #60A5FA. focus.ring-opacity = 0.25. Alpha-composited over white (#FFFFFF): result colour #D7E9FE, contrast 1.24:1. At full opacity #60A5FA on #FFFFFF = 2.54:1. Both values fail WCAG SC 1.4.11 (minimum 3:1).
fix_suggestion: Replace focus.ring-color with primary-600 (#1D4ED8), which achieves 6.70:1 on white. Set focus.ring-opacity to 1 or eliminate it. In dark mode, retain primary-400 (achieves 7.02:1 on #0F172A). Alternatively apply a dual-layer focus indicator: 2px primary-700 outline plus 2px white offset ring.

title: Package exports map points to .ts source files — broken for all non-TypeScript consumers
impact: Any consumer outside this Turborepo monorepo (Drupal CDN integration, plain Node.js scripts, standard npm install) will receive a package whose entry points are non-executable .ts files. The files field publishes dist/ and src/tokens.json but NOT src/*.ts — so exports map targets are missing from the published artifact. Compiled dist/index.js, dist/css.js, dist/lit.js, dist/utils.js all exist and are correct but are entirely unreachable.
evidence: exports['.'].import = './src/index.ts', exports['./css'].import = './src/css.ts', etc. files field = ['dist', 'src/tokens.json'] — src/*.ts not included. The compiled dist/ files exist and are correct but no export condition references them.
fix_suggestion: Update all export conditions to point to dist artifacts: { '.': { types: './dist/index.d.ts', import: './dist/index.js' }, './css': { types: './dist/css.d.ts', import: './dist/css.js' }, './lit': { types: './dist/lit.d.ts', import: './dist/lit.js' }, './utils': { types: './dist/utils.d.ts', import: './dist/utils.js' } }. Update top-level main and types fields accordingly.

title: text.on-secondary (white on secondary-500) fails WCAG SC 1.4.3 at 3.68:1
impact: Normal text rendered with text.on-secondary on a secondary-500 background (badges, chips, filled secondary buttons) fails WCAG 2.1 SC 1.4.3. In healthcare, secondary colours may be used for status indicators — low-vision users cannot reliably read this text.
evidence: color.text.on-secondary = var(--hx-color-neutral-0) = #FFFFFF. secondary-500 = #0891B2. Calculated contrast: 3.68:1. WCAG SC 1.4.3 requires 4.5:1 for normal text. FAIL by 0.82 ratio points.
fix_suggestion: Replace text.on-secondary with var(--hx-color-neutral-900) (#0F172A on secondary-500 = 9.04:1, PASS). Or darken background reference to secondary-700 (#155E75) where white achieves 7.52:1.

title: text.on-success (white on success-500) fails WCAG SC 1.4.3 at 3.30:1
impact: Normal text using text.on-success on success-500 backgrounds (confirmation banners, positive-state indicators) fails WCAG 2.1 SC 1.4.3. In healthcare, success states may indicate medication confirmation — low-vision users cannot reliably read these messages.
evidence: color.text.on-success = var(--hx-color-neutral-0) = #FFFFFF. success-500 = #16A34A. Calculated contrast: 3.30:1. WCAG SC 1.4.3 minimum 4.5:1. FAIL by 1.20 ratio points.
fix_suggestion: Replace text.on-success with var(--hx-color-neutral-900) (#0F172A on success-500 = 9.13:1, PASS). If white text is required, minimum-passing background is success-600 (#15803D) at 4.72:1.

title: text.on-info (white on info-500) fails WCAG SC 1.4.3 at 4.10:1
impact: Normal text using text.on-info on info-500 backgrounds (informational banners, info-state chips) fails WCAG 2.1 SC 1.4.3. Informational messages in healthcare may carry clinical guidance — low-vision users cannot reliably read them.
evidence: color.text.on-info = var(--hx-color-neutral-0) = #FFFFFF. info-500 = #0284C7. Calculated contrast: 4.10:1. WCAG SC 1.4.3 minimum 4.5:1. FAIL by 0.40 ratio points.
fix_suggestion: Replace text.on-info with var(--hx-color-neutral-900) (#0F172A on info-500 = 7.04:1, PASS). Or reference info-600 (#0369A1) as background where white achieves 5.24:1.

title: color.accent and color.warning are identical across all 11 scale steps — copy-paste error
impact: Every consumer using --hx-color-accent-* gets amber/warning amber instead of a distinct accent colour. Accent and warning states are visually indistinguishable, violating WCAG 1.4.1 (Use of Color). In healthcare dashboards where warning states signal clinical alerts, this is a patient-safety risk.
evidence: lines 29-41 (accent) and 69-81 (warning) are byte-for-byte identical across all 11 scales: accent-500 = warning-500 = #D97706, accent-950 = warning-950 = #451A03, etc. Runtime comparison confirmed: JSON.stringify(tokens.color.accent) === JSON.stringify(tokens.color.warning) is true.
fix_suggestion: Define a distinct accent colour ramp separate from warning. A purple/violet scale (Tailwind violet: 50=#F5F3FF, 500=#7C3AED, 950=#2E1065) is a common complement to a blue primary. This is a breaking change requiring a semver major bump and changeset entry.

title: easing.default and easing.in-out share identical cubic-bezier values — easing.in-out is semantically wrong
impact: Components using --hx-easing-in-out for bidirectional transitions (accordion, drawer open/close) get asymmetric fast-out-slow-in motion instead of true symmetric in-out. In a healthcare clinical UI, motion artifacts distract clinicians.
evidence: easing.default.value = 'cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1)'. easing['in-out'].value = 'cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1)'. Both values confirmed identical by script. Standard CSS ease-in-out = cubic-bezier(0.42, 0, 0.58, 1).
fix_suggestion: Correct easing.in-out to cubic-bezier(0.42, 0, 0.58, 1) (standard symmetric in-out) or cubic-bezier(0.445, 0.05, 0.55, 0.95) (Penner ease-in-out). Breaking change — components consuming --hx-easing-in-out will change animation behaviour.

title: color.focus-ring and focus.ring-color are duplicate tokens that diverge in dark mode
impact: Components must choose between --hx-color-focus-ring and --hx-focus-ring-color. Both resolve to primary-400 in light mode, but only --hx-color-focus-ring is overridden in dark mode. Components using --hx-focus-ring-color retain the light-mode value in dark mode, causing theming inconsistency across the component library.
evidence: tokens.json line 137: color.focus-ring = var(--hx-color-primary-400) → --hx-color-focus-ring. tokens.json lines 267-272: focus.ring-color = var(--hx-color-primary-400) → --hx-focus-ring-color. Dark mode (line 385) overrides --hx-color-focus-ring but has no override for --hx-focus-ring-color. Confirmed divergence in dark mode.
fix_suggestion: Remove color.focus-ring from tokens.json. All components should use --hx-focus-ring-color (from focus.ring-color) as the single canonical token. Add dark.focus.ring-color to the dark section. This is a breaking change for consumers explicitly overriding --hx-color-focus-ring.

title: Dark mode missing all on-color text token overrides (on-primary, on-secondary, on-error, on-success, on-warning, on-info)
impact: In dark mode, if any surface background changes for dark theming, the on-colour text tokens will not update correspondingly. Additionally, color.text has no on-accent token in either mode. The three light-mode on-colour failures (on-secondary, on-success, on-info) propagate to dark mode unchanged.
evidence: dark.color.text section (lines 361-389) contains keys: primary, secondary, muted, disabled, inverse, link, link-hover, link-visited, link-active. Keys on-primary, on-secondary, on-error, on-success, on-warning, on-info are absent. color.text also has no on-accent key.
fix_suggestion: Add on-color overrides under dark.color.text for all six semantic states. Fix the three failing light-mode on-colour pairs first (on-secondary, on-success, on-info — see separate issues). Add color.text.on-accent = var(--hx-color-neutral-900) in light mode (neutral-900 on accent-500 achieves passing contrast).

title: Zero test files in @helix/tokens — package entirely untested and invisible to Turborepo test pipeline
impact: 12 components in hx-library import tokenStyles from @helix/tokens/lit. The admin dashboard imports getTokenStats, getContrastColor, getColorSubgroups, getTokensByPrefix. The docs token-explorer imports resolveTokenRef. All consumers depend on logic with zero test coverage. Turborepo silently skips this package — the CLAUDE.md 100% test pass gate is structurally broken for this package.
evidence: find /workspace/packages/hx-tokens -name '*.test.*' -o -name '*.spec.*' returns no output. package.json scripts: { build, type-check, clean } — no test key. No vitest.config.ts exists. turbo.json has a test task but hx-tokens has no test script to satisfy it.
fix_suggestion: Create vitest.config.ts using environment: 'node' (no browser APIs used). Add 'test': 'vitest run --coverage' to package.json scripts. Add vitest and @vitest/coverage-v8 to devDependencies. Minimum tests needed: flattenTokens output shape, tokenMap key integrity, all utils functions, CSS string structure, dark mode CSS block generation.

title: getContrastColor produces wrong output for 3-char hex — runtime confirmed bug
impact: getContrastColor is called by the admin dashboard colors page and the docs token explorer to determine black/white text for colour swatches. For 3-char hex input (#fff, #000), parseInt of an empty substring returns NaN. Luminance becomes NaN. NaN > 0.5 is false, so '#ffffff' (white text) is always returned — meaning #000 incorrectly returns white text instead of black text.
evidence: utils.ts lines 66-68: clean.substring(4, 6) returns '' for 3-char hex; parseInt('', 16) = NaN. isHexColor() (line 43) regex /^#[0-9a-fA-F]{3,8}$/ explicitly accepts 3-8 char hex, creating a false contract with getContrastColor. Runtime test confirmed: getContrastColor('#fff') = '#ffffff' (incorrect).
fix_suggestion: Either (a) expand 3-char hex before processing (#abc → #aabbcc), handle 4-char (#rgba → ignore alpha), and strip alpha from 8-char inputs; or (b) constrain isHexColor to only accept 6-char hex (/^#[0-9a-fA-F]{6}$/) to match what getContrastColor can process. Add input guard: if (!isHexColor(hex)) throw new Error.

title: lit.ts uses css() TemplateStringsArray double-cast — bypasses Lit's CSS injection safeguard
impact: Lit's css() tagged template enforces that CSS originates from static template strings to prevent injection. The as unknown as TemplateStringsArray cast bypasses this contract. If tokens.json is ever compromised (supply chain attack, unauthorized commit), malicious CSS values like '} :host { color: red; } /*' could escape the :host block and inject rules into shadow DOM. The correct API for dynamic strings is unsafeCSS(), which is explicit about the trade-off.
evidence: Lines 5-7 and 12-15: css([`...dynamic...`] as unknown as TemplateStringsArray). The as unknown as chain exists specifically because TypeScript rejects the direct cast — a signal the usage violates the API contract. Token values are interpolated directly with no sanitization. Runtime: Lit 3.x production build does not throw, but dev builds may.
fix_suggestion: Replace css([...] as unknown as TemplateStringsArray) with unsafeCSS() from 'lit': import { unsafeCSS } from 'lit'; export const tokenStyles = unsafeCSS(`:host { ${tokenEntries.map(t => `  ${t.name}: ${t.value};`).join('\n')} }`). Add a comment: '// unsafeCSS is appropriate here: values are statically derived from tokens.json, not user input'.

title: groupBy casts entry[field] as string — silently corrupts path (string[]) and description (string|undefined) fields
impact: TokenEntry.path is string[] and description is string|undefined. If groupBy is called with field='path', the key becomes the string representation of an array ('color,primary,500'), producing nonsense groupings. If called with field='description', undefined coerces to the string 'undefined'. TypeScript cannot catch this at call sites because the signature accepts any keyof TokenEntry.
evidence: Line 38: 'const key = entry[field] as string'. TokenEntry.path (types.ts line 12) = string[]; description (line 13) = string|undefined. Runtime confirmed: entry['description'] returns undefined; entry['path'] returns an array.
fix_suggestion: Constrain the field parameter: field: keyof Pick<TokenEntry, 'name' | 'value' | 'category' | 'group' | 'key'>. Remove the cast — the constrained type is already string. Or use a generic: function groupBy<K extends string>(entries: TokenEntry[], fn: (e: TokenEntry) => K): Record<K, TokenEntry[]>.

title: isTokenDefinition type guard does not verify value is a string — non-string values silently pass
impact: The guard returns true for any object with a 'value' property regardless of its type. { value: 42 } or { value: null } passes and is stored in TokenEntry.value (typed as string) without any runtime assertion. Any non-conforming token structure silently produces a corrupted TokenEntry that propagates to all consumers.
evidence: Line 7: 'return typeof obj === 'object' && obj !== null && 'value' in obj' — no typeof check on the value property itself. Runtime confirmed: isTokenDefinition({ value: 42 }) returns true. TokenDefinition.value is typed as string (types.ts line 2).
fix_suggestion: Strengthen the guard: return typeof obj === 'object' && obj !== null && 'value' in obj && typeof (obj as Record<string, unknown>)['value'] === 'string'. This accurately reflects what TokenDefinition requires.

title: Missing sideEffects: false prevents tree-shaking of @helix/tokens
impact: Without sideEffects: false, bundlers cannot eliminate unused exports. A consumer importing only @helix/tokens/utils (pure functions, no Lit) will cause the bundler to retain the entire module graph including lit.ts, pulling in the Lit runtime (~5KB min+gz). For Drupal CDN scenarios where only CSS tokens are needed, this forces a hard Lit dependency.
evidence: package.json has no sideEffects field. dist/utils.js is 2,306 bytes with zero runtime dependencies. dist/lit.js imports from 'lit'. Without sideEffects: false, conservative bundlers treat all exports as potentially side-effectful.
fix_suggestion: Add "sideEffects": false to package.json. All module-level work produces only named exports with no DOM mutations, event listeners, or global registrations. The only genuine side-effect artifact is tokens.css: "sideEffects": ["./dist/tokens.css"].

title: lit declared as runtime dependency — forces Lit transitive dependency on all consumers regardless of import subpath
impact: Any package declaring @helix/tokens as a dependency installs Lit as a transitive runtime dependency even if it only consumes ./utils or ./css (which import no Lit). hx-library already has Lit as a direct dependency — adding it again via @helix/tokens risks duplicate Lit instances and broken CSSResult sharing between tokenStyles and component styles.
evidence: 'dependencies': { 'lit': '^3.3.2' }. Lit is imported only in src/lit.ts (the ./lit subpath). All other entry points (index.ts, css.ts, utils.ts) have zero Lit imports. Verified in source files.
fix_suggestion: Move lit from dependencies to peerDependencies: { 'lit': '>=3.0.0' } with peerDependenciesMeta: { 'lit': { 'optional': true } }. This marks it optional (only required for the ./lit subpath) and prevents duplicate instances.

title: Build script uses npx tsx instead of locally installed tsx — non-deterministic CI builds
impact: npx tsx downloads tsx from the npm registry if not cached, or uses a cached version that may differ from the devDependency pin. CI runners on cold caches or different environments may execute generate-css.ts against a different tsx version than declared, producing non-reproducible builds. In a healthcare enterprise context, build reproducibility is required for audit trails.
evidence: scripts.build = 'tsc && npx tsx scripts/generate-css.ts'. tsx@^4.19.0 is in devDependencies and is available at ./node_modules/.bin/tsx after npm ci.
fix_suggestion: Change to: 'tsc && tsx scripts/generate-css.ts'. npm scripts automatically resolve binaries from ./node_modules/.bin, using the pinned version deterministically.

title: CI matrix tests Node.js 18 despite root package.json engines field requiring >=20
impact: CI actively runs and reports passing builds on a Node version explicitly declared unsupported. This creates false confidence in Node 18 compatibility and may mask runtime behaviour differences. The fail-fast: false setting means other matrix jobs continue even if Node 18 fails, obscuring root causes.
evidence: package.json: 'engines': { 'node': '>=20.0.0' }. ci-matrix.yml line 33: node-version: [18, 20, 22]. Node 18 is below the declared minimum.
fix_suggestion: Remove Node 18 from the matrix: node-version: [20, 22]. If Node 18 compatibility is intentionally desired, remove or update the engines field in package.json to reflect it.

title: size.touch-target = 2.75rem meets WCAG 2.5.5 at exactly the minimum — no tolerance for sub-16px root font sizes
impact: At 16px root font size: 2.75rem = 44px — exactly at the WCAG 2.5.5 AA minimum. At 15px root: 41.25px (FAIL). At 14px root: 38.5px (FAIL). Enterprise dashboards commonly reduce root font size. Clinical tablets and kiosks using reduced font sizes would drop below the WCAG minimum.
evidence: size.touch-target = '2.75rem'. WCAG 2.5.5 requires 44px CSS pixels, not 44px rem. At any root font size below 16px, this token falls below the minimum.
fix_suggestion: Change size.touch-target to '3rem' (48px at 16px root, provides a safety margin, aligns with iOS HIG 44pt recommendation). For critical healthcare kiosk paths, supplement with min-height using px fallback.

title: duration.reduced undocumented as the canonical prefers-reduced-motion value
impact: duration.reduced = '0ms' and duration.instant = '0ms' are semantically identical with no documentation distinguishing them. Component authors may unknowingly use duration.fast or duration.normal inside @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) instead of duration.reduced, violating WCAG SC 2.3.3. The transition.* tokens have no corresponding reduced variants.
evidence: Line 248: duration.instant = '0ms'. Line 253: duration.reduced = '0ms'. No description field on either token. transition.fast/normal/slow have no reduced counterparts.
fix_suggestion: Add description to duration.reduced: 'Use inside @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) blocks. Never use duration.fast/normal/slow for animated properties without a reduced-motion override.' Add transition.reduced: { value: '0ms ease' } to complete the reduced-motion swap set.

title: resolveTokenRef regex only resolves first var() per iteration — multi-var values partially unresolved
impact: String.match() without the 'g' flag returns only the first match. Each of the 5 maxDepth iterations resolves at most one var() reference. A value with two independent var() references (e.g., 'var(--a) var(--b)') only resolves --a in iteration 1 and --b in iteration 2. With 5 iterations, values with more than 5 distinct parallel references remain partially unresolved silently.
evidence: Line 54: const match = resolved.match(/var\((--[\w-]+)(?:,\s*([^)]+))?\)/) — no 'g' flag. Runtime test: resolveTokenRef('0 1px var(--hx-a), 0 2px var(--hx-b)', map, 1) returns '0 1px #111, 0 2px var(--hx-b)' — second var() not resolved with maxDepth=1.
fix_suggestion: Replace match-and-replace with global replacement: resolved = resolved.replace(/var\((--[\w-]+)(?:,\s*([^)]+))?\)/g, (fullMatch, varName, fallback) => tokenMap[varName] ?? fallback?.trim() ?? fullMatch) inside a depth-limited while loop that breaks when the string stops changing.

title: Duplicate flatten logic between src/index.ts and scripts/generate-css.ts — DRY violation
impact: Both files implement the same path-joining algorithm ('--hx-' + path.join('-')) and identical recursive tree-walking. Any future change to token naming conventions must be applied in two places. The script also duplicates TokenDefinition as a local TokenDef interface. The generate-css.ts script is excluded from type-checking (see separate issue), making drift between the two implementations undetectable.
evidence: generate-css.ts line 22: name = '--hx-${path.join('-')}' is identical to index.ts line 19. Both check 'value' in val and recurse. generate-css.ts declares TokenDef (lines 7-9) duplicating TokenDefinition from types.ts.
fix_suggestion: Export a low-level flatten utility from src/utils.ts returning {name, value} pairs. Import it in generate-css.ts. Or import tokenEntries directly from the built src output after the tsc step completes, since the build runs 'tsc && tsx scripts/generate-css.ts'.

title: generate-css.ts has no error handling around readFileSync or JSON.parse
impact: If tokens.json is missing (after clean), contains merge conflict markers, or is malformed JSON, the build script throws a raw Node.js stack trace with no actionable message. In CI, this produces a red build with opaque output and no indication of the actual cause.
evidence: Lines 33-34: const tokens = JSON.parse(readFileSync(jsonPath, 'utf-8')) — no surrounding try/catch. The script is also excluded from tsconfig type-checking, so type errors are invisible to npm run type-check.
fix_suggestion: Wrap lines 33-34 in try/catch: try { ... } catch (e) { console.error('[generate-css] Failed to read/parse tokens.json:', e instanceof Error ? e.message : e); process.exit(1); }

title: tokensJson cast to Record<string, unknown> discards the precise typed JSON import
impact: TypeScript infers a precise type for tokensJson from resolveJsonModule. The cast discards this type, meaning TypeScript cannot catch a rename of the 'dark' key in tokens.json — darkJson would silently be undefined with no compile error. The two subsequent casts (darkJson as Record<string, unknown> on line 58) are downstream consequences.
evidence: Line 51: const { dark: darkJson, ...lightJson } = tokensJson as Record<string, unknown>. The JSON import type has a precise shape — casting to Record<string, unknown> is a workaround rather than a solution.
fix_suggestion: Remove the cast. Access the dark key directly: const { dark: darkJson, ...lightJson } = tokensJson. If the inferred type does not match, define an explicit interface for the tokens.json shape or use the 'satisfies' operator to validate without widening.

title: Token values injected into CSS strings without schema validation — CSS injection surface in generate-css.ts, css.ts, and lit.ts
impact: Token values from tokens.json are interpolated directly into CSS output across three code paths with no validation. A compromised tokens.json (supply chain attack, unauthorized commit) could inject arbitrary CSS. Example: a value like 'red; } body { color: blue; }' would break the CSS block structure in the generated stylesheet consumed by all Drupal healthcare applications.
evidence: generate-css.ts lines 49-51: lines.push('  ${t.name}: ${t.value};') — no sanitization. css.ts lines 4-6: same pattern. lit.ts lines 6, 13-14: same pattern. Three independent injection surfaces with no shared validation.
fix_suggestion: Implement schema validation for tokens.json at build time. Validate all token values are legitimate CSS values using a strict allowlist or CSS grammar validator. Reject values containing '{', '}', '/*', '*/', or other CSS syntax characters that could escape property value context. Centralize validation in src/index.ts so all three consumers benefit.

title: transition tokens hardcode 'ease' keyword — disconnected from the easing token system
impact: transition.fast/normal/slow all use the literal 'ease' keyword instead of referencing var(--hx-easing-default). Consumers overriding --hx-easing-default for theming will have no effect on components using --hx-transition-* tokens. The easing and transition token systems are disconnected, breaking the cascade architecture.
evidence: tokens.json lines 263-265: transition.fast = '150ms ease', transition.normal = '250ms ease', transition.slow = '350ms ease'. The easing token system at lines 255-261 defines 5 distinct cubic-bezier curves that are unused by the transition shortcuts.
fix_suggestion: Rewrite as: transition.fast = '150ms var(--hx-easing-default)', transition.normal = '250ms var(--hx-easing-default)', transition.slow = '350ms var(--hx-easing-default)'. CSS custom properties in transition shorthand timing-function position are valid CSS.

title: opacity.disabled and opacity.overlay hardcode values that duplicate scale tokens — theming cascade broken
impact: opacity.disabled (0.5) equals opacity['50'] (0.5). opacity.overlay (0.75) equals opacity['75'] (0.75). Components using --hx-opacity-50 instead of --hx-opacity-disabled won't respond to a consumer override of --hx-opacity-disabled. Theming is fragmented across two parallel token paths.
evidence: Line 330: opacity.disabled = '0.5'. opacity['50'] = '0.5' (line 326). Line 331: opacity.overlay = '0.75'. opacity['75'] = '0.75' (line 327). Script confirmed: duplicate 50/disabled = true, duplicate 75/overlay = true.
fix_suggestion: Make semantic tokens reference scale tokens: opacity.disabled.value = 'var(--hx-opacity-50)', opacity.overlay.value = 'var(--hx-opacity-75)'. This creates proper cascade relationships with a single source of truth.

title: breakpoint and container token categories duplicate four identical pixel values
impact: breakpoint.sm/md/lg/xl = container.sm/md/lg/xl (640px, 768px, 1024px, 1280px). Any breakpoint update requires changing both locations — divergence is inevitable. The schemas are also inconsistent: container has no 2xl, breakpoint has 2xl (1536px).
evidence: tokens.json lines 293-299 (container) and lines 347-353 (breakpoint) confirm: sm, md, lg, xl values are byte-for-byte identical across both categories.
fix_suggestion: Have container tokens reference breakpoint tokens: container.sm.value = 'var(--hx-breakpoint-sm)', container.md.value = 'var(--hx-breakpoint-md)', etc. Retain container.content (72rem) as its own value. Add container.2xl = 'var(--hx-breakpoint-2xl)' for consistency.

title: Dark mode missing focus.ring-color override — --hx-focus-ring-color diverges from --hx-color-focus-ring in dark mode
impact: Dark mode overrides --hx-color-focus-ring (color.focus-ring) to primary-400, but --hx-focus-ring-color (focus.ring-color) has no dark override. Components using --hx-focus-ring-color will retain the light-mode value in dark mode, creating inconsistent focus ring colour depending on which token a component uses.
evidence: dark section (lines 360-402) contains color, body, shadow subgroups — no 'focus' key. dist/tokens.css dark block shows --hx-color-focus-ring overridden but no --hx-focus-ring-color override.
fix_suggestion: Add dark.focus.ring-color to the dark section matching dark.color.focus-ring value. This is a temporary fix — the correct long-term fix is consolidating to a single focus ring colour token (see the color.focus-ring duplication issue).

title: Missing healthcare-specific clinical status tokens — error/warning/success insufficient for clinical alert states
impact: Clinical UIs require semantic tokens for patient alert states: critical (life-threatening), high (urgent), moderate (caution), normal, low. Repurposing generic error/warning/success for these states is insufficient — a critical vital sign alert (SpO2 < 90%) must be visually distinct from a form validation error. Without dedicated clinical status tokens, component authors will hardcode clinical colours, creating inconsistency across healthcare applications.
evidence: Color scales present: [primary, secondary, accent, neutral, success, warning, error, info]. No color.status.*, color.clinical.*, or color.alert.* category exists. CLAUDE.md explicitly identifies this as an enterprise healthcare library serving organizations where software failures impact patient care.
fix_suggestion: Add a color.status category: { critical: {}, high: {}, moderate: {}, normal: {}, low: {} } with distinct colour scales. Add text.on-status.* pairing tokens. Consider aligning with HL7 FHIR observation interpretation codes (HH, H, N, L, LL) for naming inspiration.

title: dist/tokens.json is an unverified duplicate of src/tokens.json — drift is possible
impact: tsconfig.json includes src/**/*.json, causing tsc to copy src/tokens.json to dist/tokens.json. Two independent copies of the token source of truth exist with no CI assertion they are identical. If src/tokens.json is edited and the build is cached, dist/tokens.json can drift. The generate-css.ts script reads from src/tokens.json, so a drift would produce CSS inconsistent with the dist copy.
evidence: tsconfig.json include: ['src/**/*.ts', 'src/**/*.json']. Both src/tokens.json and dist/tokens.json confirmed present. No CI check asserts their equality.
fix_suggestion: Remove src/**/*.json from tsconfig.json include. JSON files are resolved via resolveJsonModule at import time and do not need to be compiled. Then dist/tokens.json ceases to exist as a concern. The exports map ./tokens.json entry correctly points to src/tokens.json.

title: Changeset config links private @helix/tokens with @helix/library — publish workflow conflict
impact: Changesets will attempt to version both packages together. Since @helix/tokens has private: true, changesets skips the publish step but still records the version bump. This creates a mismatch between what changesets records and what is actually published, potentially corrupting release changelogs.
evidence: .changeset/config.json: 'linked': [['@helix/library', '@helix/tokens']]. packages/hx-tokens/package.json: 'private': true. Changesets respects private: true and skips publishing but still versions.
fix_suggestion: Remove @helix/tokens from the linked array since it is private and not published. Use updateInternalDependencies: 'patch' to keep workspace references in sync automatically.

title: Turborepo build outputs missing tsconfig.tsbuildinfo — incremental TypeScript compilation cache not restored
impact: Both hx-tokens and hx-library use TypeScript composite projects (composite: true). tsc writes tsconfig.tsbuildinfo for incremental rebuilds. turbo.json declares outputs: ['dist/**', '.astro/**', '.next/**'] — this excludes *.tsbuildinfo. On every Turbo cache restore, tsc performs a full recompilation instead of incremental, negating significant CI speed benefit from remote caching.
evidence: turbo.json build.outputs: ['dist/**', '.astro/**', '.next/**']. packages/hx-tokens/tsconfig.json: 'composite': true. tsconfig.tsbuildinfo exists on disk (46906 bytes) but is not declared as a Turbo output.
fix_suggestion: Add tsconfig.tsbuildinfo to build outputs: 'outputs': ['dist/**', 'tsconfig.tsbuildinfo', '.astro/**', '.next/**']. Or use a glob: 'packages/**/*.tsbuildinfo'.

title: scripts/ directory excluded from tsconfig — generate-css.ts is never type-checked
impact: generate-css.ts is the production build script that generates dist/tokens.css — the primary Drupal CDN artifact consumed by all healthcare applications. Type errors in this script are invisible to npm run type-check and CI Gate 1. The script contains the same redundant cast pattern found in index.ts (val as TokenDef after inline guard) but this goes undetected.
evidence: tsconfig.json exclude: ['node_modules', 'dist', 'scripts']. scripts/generate-css.ts is executed at build time via tsx (no type-checking). generate-css.ts line 22: (val as TokenDef).value — redundant cast after inline guard on line 19.
fix_suggestion: Create tsconfig.scripts.json extending the base config with include: ['scripts/**/*.ts']. Update package.json type-check script: 'tsc --noEmit && tsc -p tsconfig.scripts.json --noEmit'.

title: Missing README.md — npm package has no documentation
impact: npm registry shows 'No readme found'. Developers cannot understand the package's purpose, API surface, or token structure without reading source code. This significantly increases onboarding time and reduces adoption.
evidence: Directory listing: dist/, package.json, scripts/, src/, tsconfig.json, tsconfig.tsbuildinfo — no README.md present.
fix_suggestion: Create README.md covering: package description, installation, import paths (main/css/lit/utils/tokens.css/tokens.json), usage examples for each entry point, token structure overview, and dark mode usage patterns.

title: No lint script in package.json and hx-tokens dist/ not excluded from root ESLint config
impact: Two issues: (1) Without a lint script, Turborepo silently skips linting for hx-tokens. (2) The root eslint.config.js ignores 'dist/**' relative to the workspace root and 'packages/hx-library/dist/**' explicitly, but 'packages/hx-tokens/dist/**' is absent. Generated .js files in packages/hx-tokens/dist/ are actively linted on every npm run lint.
evidence: package.json scripts: { build, type-check, clean } — no lint entry. eslint.config.js ignores: ['dist/**', 'packages/hx-library/dist/**']. packages/hx-tokens/dist/ is not excluded.
fix_suggestion: Add 'lint': 'eslint src/' to package.json scripts. Add 'packages/hx-tokens/dist/**' to the root eslint.config.js ignores array.

title: text.disabled and dark.color.text.disabled have intentionally low contrast — WCAG exemption undocumented
impact: text.disabled (#94A3B8 on white = 2.56:1) and dark.color.text.disabled (#475569 on #0F172A = 2.36:1) fall below 4.5:1. WCAG SC 1.4.3 exempts inactive/disabled components. The exemption only holds if components apply disabled semantics correctly (aria-disabled or native disabled attribute). No documentation of the exemption exists on these tokens.
evidence: color.text.disabled = var(--hx-color-neutral-400) = #94A3B8. On white: 2.56:1. dark.color.text.disabled = var(--hx-color-neutral-600) = #475569. On #0F172A: 2.36:1. Both below 4.5:1 but WCAG 1.4.3 provides an explicit exemption for inactive UI components.
fix_suggestion: Add description fields: { 'value': 'var(--hx-color-neutral-400)', 'description': 'WCAG SC 1.4.3 exempt: use ONLY for aria-disabled=true or native disabled UI components. Never use for active text.' } Add design token linting rules to enforce this constraint.

title: Redundant as TokenDefinition cast after isTokenDefinition type guard
impact: The type guard at line 16 already narrows val to TokenDefinition. The intermediate variable and cast on line 17 are redundant noise that establish a pattern of preferring casts over type guards, degrading code quality.
evidence: Line 16: if (isTokenDefinition(val)) { — val is already TokenDefinition in this block. Line 17: const def = val as TokenDefinition — unnecessary. All uses of def.value and def.description could use val directly.
fix_suggestion: Remove the intermediate variable. Replace def.value with val.value and def.description with val.description directly. Trust the type guard.

title: TokenEntry.description typed as string | undefined but flattenTokens always sets it to string via nullish coalescing
impact: Consumers reading the interface (string | undefined) must null-check description before use, even though at runtime it is always a non-undefined string. With exactOptionalPropertyTypes enabled in tsconfig.base.json, this mismatch is material. Misleading API contract for consumers.
evidence: types.ts line 13: description?: string. index.ts line 25: description: def.description ?? '' — always produces a string, never undefined.
fix_suggestion: Either change the interface to description: string (matching the runtime guarantee), or pass def.description directly without the nullish coalescing fallback and keep the optional type. Option (a) gives consumers a stronger contract and eliminates unnecessary null checks.

title: --hx-space-px sorts last in generated CSS due to JavaScript object key ordering
impact: JavaScript's Object.entries() processes integer-like string keys (0-64) in ascending numeric order before non-integer string keys. The 'px' key sorts after all numeric keys, so --hx-space-px appears after --hx-space-64 in the generated CSS. Not a functional bug, but breaks visual readability of the token sheet and may confuse tooling that assumes scale ordering.
evidence: dist/tokens.css: numeric space tokens 0-64 appear in order, then --hx-space-px appears after --hx-space-64. The 'px' key is a non-integer string; Object.entries places it after all integer-indexed keys.
fix_suggestion: Move the 'px' key to appear first in the space object in tokens.json (before '0') so it emits as --hx-space-px first in insertion order. Or rename to 'space-0-5' to keep it within the numeric sort range.

title: Missing package.json metadata: keywords, repository, homepage, author, license
impact: npm registry cannot display keywords, repository link, homepage, author, or license information. Developers cannot assess licensing compliance. npm install generates warnings about missing fields. Package is unsearchable via npm search.
evidence: package.json fields present: name, version, private, description, type, main, types, exports, files, scripts, dependencies, devDependencies. Fields absent: keywords, repository, homepage, author, license.
fix_suggestion: Add: keywords: ['design-tokens', 'css-variables', 'lit', 'web-components', 'healthcare'], repository: { type: 'git', url: '...', directory: 'packages/hx-tokens' }, license: 'MIT' (or applicable), author: 'organization name'.

title: No engines field in hx-tokens package.json despite monorepo requiring Node >=20
impact: The root package.json declares engines: { node: '>=20.0.0' } but hx-tokens/package.json has no engines field. Consumers or tooling reading the package's own engines constraint see no restriction, potentially allowing installation on Node 18 where some ESM and import.meta.url behaviours differ.
evidence: packages/hx-tokens/package.json has no engines field. Root package.json: 'engines': { 'node': '>=20.0.0' }. scripts/generate-css.ts uses fileURLToPath(import.meta.url) — available in Node 18+, but the broader monorepo requires Node 20.
fix_suggestion: Add 'engines': { 'node': '>=20.0.0' } to packages/hx-tokens/package.json to match the root constraint.

title: No Turborepo remote cache configuration in CI pipeline
impact: ci.yml runs turbo build, type-check, test, lint but does not set TURBO_TOKEN or TURBO_TEAM environment variables and does not configure Vercel Remote Cache. Every CI run performs a full cold build with no cache hits, increasing CI cycle time for all contributors.
evidence: No TURBO_TOKEN, TURBO_TEAM, or turbo remote cache configuration in ci.yml or ci-matrix.yml. No .turbo/config.json found in workspace root.
fix_suggestion: Add TURBO_TOKEN and TURBO_TEAM to GitHub Actions secrets. Configure turbo commands with: --team=${{ vars.TURBO_TEAM }} --token=${{ secrets.TURBO_TOKEN }}. Or add a .turbo/config.json with team slug and reference TURBO_TOKEN via env block.

title: Dark token CSS string built twice in memory — 1,626 bytes of redundant allocation in css.ts
impact: darkMediaCSS and darkManualCSS both iterate darkTokenEntries to produce the same 27 CSS declarations, differing only in their selector wrapper. The 27 declarations (1,626 bytes) are duplicated in memory. fullTokensCSS then holds all three strings, repeating the dark content a third time. Minor memory cost at current token count but scales linearly.
evidence: Measured: tokensCSS = 10,093 bytes, darkMediaCSS = 1,731 bytes, darkManualCSS = 1,626 bytes, fullTokensCSS = 13,452 bytes. The 1,626-byte declaration block appears in both darkMediaCSS and darkManualCSS as separate string allocations.
fix_suggestion: Extract the dark declarations into a shared local string: const darkDecls = darkTokenEntries.map(t => '    ${t.name}: ${t.value};').join('\n'). Compose darkMediaCSS and darkManualCSS by wrapping darkDecls rather than re-mapping the array.

title: Eager module-level evaluation in index.ts is correct but undocumented — maintenance risk
impact: All four exported data structures are computed synchronously at import time. For 274 tokens this is sub-millisecond and architecturally correct — tokens are static data that never changes at runtime. Without a comment documenting this intent, a future developer may attempt to 'optimize' with lazy getters, adding complexity with no benefit.
evidence: flattenTokens called at line 54-58, groupBy at line 62, Object.fromEntries at line 65 — all at module scope. O(n) traversal for ~300 tokens is negligible (~0.1ms). Eager evaluation is the correct pattern for static immutable data.
fix_suggestion: Add a comment above the module-level declarations: '// Eagerly evaluated: tokens are immutable static data. Pre-computing derived structures at import time is intentional — lazy evaluation would add complexity with no runtime benefit.'

title: resolveTokenRef regex truncates var() fallbacks that are themselves var() references
impact: The regex [^)]+ in the fallback capture group stops at the first ')'. A fallback like var(--hx-foo, var(--hx-bar)) would capture 'var(--hx-bar' (missing closing paren) as the fallback. No current token uses this pattern, but the public API has no documentation of this limitation.
evidence: Regex: /var\((--[\w-]+)(?:,\s*([^)]+))?\)/. For input 'var(--hx-foo, var(--hx-bar))', the fallback group captures 'var(--hx-bar' (truncated). Confirmed: 0 tokens in current tokens.json use nested var() as a fallback.
fix_suggestion: Add a JSDoc @remarks note: 'Nested var() as fallback values are not supported. Fallback must be a plain CSS value.' Or expand the regex to handle one level of nested parentheses: [^)]*(?:\([^)]*\))?[^)]*
